description: A survey of the Old Testament canon, covering the Pentateuch, Historical Books, Wisdom Literature, and Prophets. Students will learn major themes, historical background, and hermeneutical principles for OT interpretation.

An introduction to pastoral counselling theories and practice. Covers cognitive-behavioural approaches, solution-focused therapy, and grief counselling within a Christian framework. Includes supervised practicum hours.

Explores the theology and practice of congregational worship. Topics include the role of music in corporate worship, song selection, leading worship teams, and integrating traditional and contemporary forms.
A practical seminar exploring Christian ministry to older adults. Topics include aging theology, dementia care, retirement transitions, grief support, and designing programmes for senior congregants.

A three-year professional degree preparing students for pastoral ministry. Core curriculum covers biblical studies, systematic theology, church history, homiletics, pastoral care, and cross-cultural mission.

An overview of the twenty-seven books of the New Testament. Students examine the life of Christ, the early church, Pauline epistles, and apocalyptic literature, with attention to historical context and theological themes.

Examines the visual arts in Christian tradition from the early church to the present. Topics include Byzantine iconography, medieval illuminated manuscripts, Reformation-era art, and contemporary Christian visual culture.

An advanced professional degree for experienced ministry practitioners. Students engage in rigorous theological reflection on ministry practice through seminars, mentoring, and a ministry project dissertation.

A weekend seminar on the Psalter as Israel's prayer book and hymnal. Participants explore lament, praise, royal, and wisdom psalms, connecting their themes to personal prayer, congregational worship, and Christian living.

Investigates theological and ethical dimensions of aging in light of Hong Kong's rapidly growing elderly population. Topics include biblical views of old age, end-of-life care, intergenerational ministry, and gerontological social policy.

Introduces the major doctrines of the Christian faith including revelation, God, creation, humanity, sin, Christology, soteriology, ecclesiology, and eschatology. Examines Lutheran confessional theology in dialogue with global Christianity.

Traces the development of sacred choral music from Gregorian chant through Lutheran chorales, Baroque oratorio, and contemporary hymnody. Students conduct and arrange sacred pieces for congregational and choral settings.

A four-year undergraduate programme providing a broad foundation in biblical studies, Christian theology, church history, and practical ministry. Equips students for lay leadership and preparation for further theological study.

A short-course seminar on integrating visual arts, drama, dance, and multimedia into congregational worship. Practical workshops cover designing liturgical art installations, writing prayers, and producing visual prayer stations.

Prepares ministry practitioners to walk alongside the dying and bereaved. Covers models of grief, cultural factors in bereavement, chaplaincy in hospital and hospice settings, and self-care for caregivers.

Examines biblical foundations and contemporary strategies for evangelism and church planting. Case studies draw from Hong Kong and diaspora Chinese contexts. Includes a field project with a local church plant.

Applies systemic family therapy theory and Christian perspectives to common family issues including marital conflict, parenting challenges, and intergenerational tensions. Students develop case conceptualisation skills.

title_en: Master of Arts in Christian Counselling
description: A professional postgraduate degree integrating psychological theory with Christian theology for clinical counselling practice. Prepares graduates for roles as pastoral counsellors, school counsellors, and licensed mental health practitioners.

title_en: The Book of Genesis: Creation, Fall, and Promise
title_zh: 創世記：創造、墮落與應許
description: Intensive seminar providing an expository study of Genesis 1–25. Examines creation accounts, the fall, the flood narrative, the Tower of Babel, and the patriarchal stories of Abraham and Isaac in their ancient Near Eastern context.

Equips worship musicians and song leaders with theological foundations and practical skills. Covers vocal technique, keyboard basics, leading congregational singing, copyright licensing, and servant leadership in worship teams.

title_en: Romans: The Gospel and Righteousness of God
title_zh: 羅馬書：上帝的福音與公義
description: A detailed exegetical and theological study of Paul's letter to the Romans. Topics include justification by faith, the righteousness of God, the role of Israel, the power of sin, the work of the Spirit, and Christian ethics.

Introduces art therapy modalities (drawing, clay, collage) as tools for spiritual care and emotional healing. Students participate in experiential workshops and reflect theologically on creativity, suffering, and restoration.

Weekend seminar focusing on Matthew 5–7. Studies the Beatitudes, Lord's Prayer, teachings on anger, lust, oaths, giving, and anxiety. Explores the ethics and spirituality of life in the Kingdom of God.

Explores the intersection of aging, health, and Christian spirituality. Topics include positive psychology and faith, spiritual practices for later life, body theology, and building age-friendly congregations.

A research-focused postgraduate degree enabling advanced study in a chosen theological discipline. Students complete coursework, language requirements, and a thesis demonstrating original scholarly research.

school_zh: 崇基學院神學組（中文大學）
description: Surveys the history of Christianity in China from the Tang Dynasty to the present. Examines inculturation, syncretism, persecution, and the growth of the Chinese church, with attention to theological reflection on gospel and culture.

school_zh: 崇基學院神學組（中文大學）
description: An exegetical study of the Revelation to John read within its Jewish apocalyptic tradition. Examines symbolic imagery, the letters to the seven churches, visions of heavenly worship, judgement, and the new creation.

title_en: Sacred Music in the Chinese Church
school_zh: 崇基學院神學組（中文大學）
description: Traces the development of church music in Chinese-speaking Christianity from missionary hymnals to contemporary Chinese worship songs. Students analyse lyrics, compose praise songs in Cantonese, and lead worship in a diverse context.

school_zh: 崇基學院神學組（中文大學）
description: A full research doctoral programme in theology and religion. Students work closely with faculty supervisors to produce an original dissertation contributing to theological scholarship. Areas include biblical studies, ethics, philosophy of religion, and church history.

title_en: Lamentations and Pastoral Response to Suffering
title_zh: 哀歌與苦難中的牧養回應
school_zh: 崇基學院神學組（中文大學）
description: A seminar exploring Lamentations as a theological resource for ministry in times of communal trauma and personal suffering. Draws connections to Hong Kong's recent social crises and develops practical approaches for pastoral care.

Introduces the grammar and syntax of Biblical Hebrew. Students learn the alphabet, vowel points, noun inflection, verb conjugation, and basic translation skills for reading narrative and poetic texts.

A close reading of Proverbs within the broader Old Testament wisdom tradition. Examines the nature of wisdom, creation theology, the fear of God, women in wisdom literature, and the relationship between wisdom and counselling.

title_zh: 基督教倫理與生命倫理
description: Applies Christian ethical frameworks to contemporary bioethical issues including beginning-of-life questions, gene editing, euthanasia, aging, organ donation, and healthcare justice. Includes case studies from the Hong Kong medical context.

An experiential seminar introducing the ancient practice of spiritual direction. Participants learn listening skills, use of silence, discernment, the Examen, and how to accompany others in their walk with God.

title_en: Master of Arts in Biblical Studies
description: A two-year postgraduate programme for in-depth study of Old and New Testaments. Students develop exegetical skills in Hebrew and Greek, engage contemporary biblical scholarship, and complete a thesis in a specialised area.

title_en: Isaiah: Servant, Salvation, and New Creation
title_zh: 以賽亞書：僕人、救恩與新創造
description: An exegetical and theological study of the book of Isaiah covering the call of the prophet, oracles of judgment and hope, the Servant Songs, Second Isaiah's message of comfort, and Third Isaiah's vision of the new creation.

A two-day residential retreat focusing on spiritual formation practices including lectio divina, contemplative prayer, journaling, and sabbath. Participants reflect on their ministry calling and spiritual health.

Prepares students for urban cross-cultural ministry in Hong Kong and beyond. Topics include urban poverty, ethnicity and migration, the ministry of presence, and developing contextualised approaches to evangelism and discipleship.

title_en: Psalms of Ascent: Pilgrimage and Community
description: Studies the fifteen Psalms of Ascent (Psalms 120–134) as songs of community pilgrimage. Explores themes of trust, blessing, unity, and hope, with applications to congregational life and personal spiritual journey.

An interdisciplinary seminar exploring Chinese ink painting as a medium for theological reflection and spiritual expression. Participants receive instruction in brush technique and create original works responding to biblical themes.

title_en: Master of Divinity with Church Music Track
title_zh: 道學碩士（教會音樂方向）
description: An M.Div. programme with a specialisation in church music ministry. Combines core ministerial training with advanced courses in music theory, choral conducting, hymnology, worship planning, and contextual worship leadership in Chinese churches.
